title: DS3231 RTC Alarm Scheduling Module
design_question: How to trigger timed events (feeding, watering, pill dispensing) accurately without a network connection?
selected_approach: DS3231 RTC module (ZS-042 breakout with CR2032 cell). Two hardware alarms configured via I²C register writes; INT/SQW pin wired to MCU external-interrupt pin to wake from deep sleep on schedule. RTClib (Adafruit) for Arduino time read/write.
rationale: DS3231 TCXO achieves ±2 ppm (±63 s/year) without calibration — far better than software timers or DS1307. Battery backup maintains schedule through power outages. Hardware alarm interrupt allows MCU deep sleep between events, minimising power consumption.

alternatives.reason_rejected: No temperature compensation; drifts up to ±20 ppm (±630 s/year). Unacceptable for daily-scheduled dosing.
Requires WiFi uptime. Suitable for cloud-connected devices but adds complexity and fails offline.

verification_notes: FACT: 'I²C Real-Time Clock — DS3231 Timekeeping and Alarms' confirms ±2 ppm accuracy, two alarms with interrupt output, and battery-backed operation.
supporting_fact_titles: I²C Real-Time Clock — DS3231 Timekeeping and Alarms
